data.title: Brainstorm and fidelity review approve success criteria that cannot be verified during sprint execution
data.description:
## Problem
The brainstorm skill (Phase 2) and its fidelity reviewers (Step 4) can produce and approve success criteria that require post-merge observation windows, dogfooding sessions, or multi-session measurement — none of which can be verified at the conclusion of the sprint skill execution workflow in a single session. This was observed during brainstorm of epic dso-khl2, where SC7 originally required a 5-business-day post-merge dogfooding observation.

The fidelity reviewers (Agent Clarity, Scope, Value) did not flag this as a measurability failure. The Value reviewer scored validation_signal at 4 despite SC7 being unverifiable within the sprint session.

## Root Cause
Neither the brainstorm skill nor the fidelity reviewer prompts enforce the hard rule that all success criteria must be verifiable during sprint skill execution in a single session. The success_measurable dimension checks for pass/fail observability but not for session-boundary constraints. The validation_signal dimension accepts post-delivery mechanisms (dogfooding, operational metrics) as valid — which they are for product validation, but not for sprint closure.

## Hard Rule
Success criteria may require adding observability that will allow measuring success after completion, but the actual measures of success must be things that can be measured during the execution of the sprint skill in a single session. SC that require post-merge observation, multi-session tracking, or external human action to verify are not acceptable as epic-level success criteria.

## Expected Fix
1. Brainstorm Phase 2 (SC authoring): Add a constraint that every SC must be verifiable by the sprint orchestrator within the same session (via test execution, grep, script output, or similar)
2. Fidelity reviewer (Agent Clarity - success_measurable dimension): Add a scoring rule that SCs requiring post-session observation score below 4
3. Fidelity reviewer (Value - validation_signal dimension): Clarify that post-delivery validation mechanisms are valid as supplementary signals but cannot be the sole validation SC — at least one SC must be session-verifiable

## Reproduction
Run /dso:brainstorm on any epic and propose an SC like "In the first sprint session after merge, observe X behavior" — the fidelity reviewers will score it 4+.
